title: Bridelia airy-shawii
intro: Bridelia airy-shawii, also known as Bridelia tomentosa, is a medicinal plant species belonging to the family Phyllanthaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in various cultures to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, due to its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Bridelia airy-shawii include flavonoids, alkaloids, and coumarins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Bridelia airy-shawii is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, or used to make ointments and salves, to treat a range of health conditions.
taxonomy: Bridelia airy-shawii is classified as a species within the family Phyllanthaceae. It belongs to the genus Bridelia, which is a group of flowering plants native to tropical and subtropical regions. The classification of Bridelia airy-shawii places it within the order Malpighiales, a group of flowering plants that are characterized by their complex leaves and showy flowers. Bridelia airy-shawii is further classified as a member of the subfamily Bridelioideae, a subfamily of flowering plants that are known for their distinctive fruit and seed characteristics. This classification helps to identify and distinguish Bridelia airy-shawii from other similar species within its genus and family.

The plant has been used to treat a range of health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal disorders. side_effects: Bridelia airy-shawii,  traditionally used in medicine, is believed to have various side effects when consumed or applied incorrectly, including skin irritation, allergic reactions, and gastrointestinal issues.
